paragraphs.body: I am Victor Liu, who is studying Politics and Philosophy at University of Pittsburgh. However, I recently took a course which I never expected it to be that useful. In this writing class, we read your excellent writing “Girl”.

After looking up the background of your childhood history, I can’t stop thinking this is such an essay which constructed with details and fabulous word choice. I can feel the emotion of that girl when reading it. The unsatisfactory of current life, her courage and determination to change empowers me a lot. “Is it true you sing benna in Sunday school?” is the sentence which lets me feel her that she is not resigned to her life. She wishes she could be part of the Sunday school and have a better future.

Even though I grow up with sufficient material circumstances, the frustration of not successfully achieve something depressed me a lot. I like to take responsibility in different facets and planning goals for myself. However, I always estimate my potential and ability wrongly, always being too confident before the start of a task. Due to the high expectation, I am always frustrated by the sharp sense of disparity when I am facing failures. This sense of disparity sometimes questions myself and forcing me to accept myself as an ordinary people.

annotations.text: Even though I grow up with sufficient material circumstances, the frustration of not successfully achieve something depressed me a lot.
annotations.comment: I am super curious about this and would like to know more about your own experiences and observations. This passage does make me wonder if you believe some people have a greater right to suffer than others? Is this suffering tied to material possessions? When we are financially comfortable, do we suffer less in other ways? All unanswerable questions, I'm sure, but you definitely got me thinking!

paragraphs.body: It is undoubtable that the situation you faced was way tougher than mine. As a new member of adulthood who faced spiritual crises but do not have to struggle with financial condition, I cannot imagine how tough your mindset is in order to letting you become successful at least in the eyes of others. Based on my understanding, rebellion would not happen without enlightenment. It is so difficult for people to jump out of the box and think in a more abstract way. Why don’t you just live under that social environment like others do? What makes you think that you need to make some changes? What property of yours that brought you here? What changes your attitude towards life the most?

annotations.text: Why don’t you just live under that social environment like others do? What makes you think that you need to make some changes? What property of yours that brought you here? What changes your attitude towards life the most?
annotations.comment: Fascinating! I hadn't thought of these questions specifically, but now I want to know the answers!

paragraphs.body: Thank you for writing this essay, it empowers me and probably improved my writing skill a little bit., I wish it does.

annotations.comment: You're doing an excellent job of engaging this text. I am curious and would love to see you explore your own experiences more and make connections between your own life and the text. Looking forward to seeing what you do next!
